# Salesforce Composite Connector - Full Documentation

# Salesforce Composite Connector 3.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Composite enables you to work with the Salesforce Batch and
SObject Tree APIs. The connector exposes convenient methods for preparing subordinate requests,
executing them in one batch, and parsing the results.

Salesforce Composite Connector also enables you to create SObject trees by using the **Create
sobject tree** operation, which is separate from the batch operations.

This connector provides DataSense for execution and to:

- Prepare subordinate requests and then parse the responses
- Create SObject trees

For compatibility information and fixed issues, see Salesforce Composite Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Salesforce Composite API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- Creating a Mule app using Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Salesforce Composite target resource
- Anypoint Studio version 7.0 or later

## Authentication Types

Salesforce Composite Connector connections use the following authentication types:

- OAuth v2.0
- OAuth Client Credentials
- OAuth JWT
- OAuth SAML
- OAuth Username and Password
- Token Connection

For information about configuring these authentication types, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Salesforce Composite Connector](salesforce-composite-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Salesforce Composite Connector 3.0 - Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## SObject Tree Operation Example

This example uses the following operations to create an SObject tree to connect to Salesforce and
transform and output the results in JSON format:

- HTTP **Listener**

  Accepts data from HTTP requests

- **Transform Message**

  Transforms the HTTP input

- **Create sobject tree**

  Connects with Salesforce and creates an SObject tree

- **Transform Message**

  Outputs the results of the **Create sobject tree** operation in JSON format

![Studio flow with an HTTP Listener component, Transform components, and a Create operation](_images/salesforce-composite-studio-use-case.png)

### Add an HTTP Listener

The HTTP **Listener** source operation initiates the flow when it detects an event in the
`createSObjectTree` path. To add the listener and trigger the flow, follow these steps:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `http` and select the **Listener** operation.
3.  Drag the **Listener** operation onto the canvas.
4.  In the Listener properties window, set the **Path** field to `/createSObjectTree`.

### Add the First Transform Message Component

The first Transform Message component uses input data to transform the HTTP input for the following
Create operation. To add the component, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for **Transform Message**.
2.  Drag the **Transform Message** component onto the canvas, to the right of **Listener**.
3.  Enter the name `Transform HTTP input for Create SObject Tree`.

The following is an example of HTTP input for the Transform Message component:

```json
{
  "records": [
    {
      "ChildAccounts": {
        "records": [
          {
            "Phone": "4321098765",
            "Website": "www.salesforce.com",
            "attributes": {
              "objectType": "Account",
              "referenceId": "ref5"
            },
            "NumberOfEmployees": "10",
            "Name": "ChildAccount1"
          }
        ]
      },
      "Contacts": {
        "records": [
          {
            "Email": "sample@salesforce.com",
            "Title": "President",
            "attributes": {
              "objectType": "Contact",
              "referenceId": "ref6"
            },
            "LastName": "Jones"
          }
        ]
      },
      "Phone": "5556543210",
      "Website": "www.salesforce.com",
      "attributes": {
        "objectType": "Account",
        "referenceId": "ref4"
      },
      "NumberOfEmployees": "101",
      "Name": "CreateSobjectTreeAccount2"
    },
    {
      "Contacts": {
        "records": [
          {
            "Email": "sample@salesforce.com",
            "Title": "President",
            "attributes": {
              "objectType": "Contact",
              "referenceId": "ref2"
            },
            "LastName": "Smith"
          },
          {
            "Email": "sample@salesforce.com",
            "Title": "Vice President",
            "attributes": {
              "objectType": "Contact",
              "referenceId": "ref3"
            },
            "LastName": "Evans"
          }
        ]
      },
      "Phone": "1234567890",
      "Website": "www.salesforce.com",
      "attributes": {
        "objectType": "Account",
        "referenceId": "ref1"
      },
      "NumberOfEmployees": "100",
      "Name": "CreateSobjectTreeAccount1"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Add the Create SObject Tree Operation

Add the Create SObject Tree operation to connect with Salesforce and create an SObject tree. To add
the operation, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `salesforce` and select **Create sobject tree**.
2.  Drag **Create sobject tree** operation to the right of **Transform Message**.
3.  Click the green plus sign (**+**) to the right of **Connector configuration** to access the
    Salesforce Composite global element configuration fields.
4.  In the **Create sobject tree** properties window, enter `Account` in the **SObject Root Type**
    field.
5.  In the properties file `src/main/mule/mule-app.properties`, declare and enter values for the
    variables in the global element.

### Add the Second Transform Message Component

The second Transform Message component transforms the output result into JSON. To add the component,
follow these steps:

1.  Drag **Transform Message** again to the right of **Create sobject tree**.
2.  Enter the following:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    %output application/json
    payload
    ---
    {
    "Id": payload.Id,
    "Fields" : ["Id", "Name"],
    "Type": "Account"
    }
    ```

### XML for the SObject Tree Operation Flow

Paste this XML code into the **Configuration XML** tab of the Anypoint Studio canvas to experiment
with the flow:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:salesforce-composite="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-composite"
xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
   xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
   xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
   xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
   http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-composite
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-composite/current/mule-salesforce-composite.xsd">
	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties"/>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
   doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="localhost" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<salesforce-composite:composite-config name="Salesforce_Composite_Salesforce_Composite"
   doc:name="Salesforce Composite Salesforce Composite">
		<salesforce-composite:oauth-user-pass-connection
      consumerKey="${consumerKey}"
      consumerSecret="${consumerSecret}"
      username="${username}"
      password="${password}"
      securityToken="${securityToken}"
      tokenEndpoint="${tokenEndpoint}" />
	</salesforce-composite:composite-config>
	<flow name="salesforce-composite-create-sobject-trees">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
      path="/createSObjectTree"
      config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform HTTP input for Create SObject Tree">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
payload.records map ((record , indexOfRecord) -> {
	"attributes": {
		"type": record.attributes.objectType,
		"referenceId": record.attributes.referenceId
	},
	"Name": record.Name,
	"Phone": record.Phone,
	"Website": record.Website,
	"NumberOfEmployees": record.NumberOfEmployees as Number,
	("ChildAccounts": {
		"records": record.ChildAccounts.records map ((record01, indexOfRecord01) -> {
			"attributes": {
				"type": record01.attributes.objectType,
				"referenceId": record01.attributes.referenceId
			},
			"Name": record01.Name,
			"Phone": record01.Phone,
			"Website": record01.Website,
			"NumberOfEmployees": record01.NumberOfEmployees as Number
		})
	}),
	"Contacts": {
		"records": record.Contacts.records map ((record01, indexOfRecord01) -> {
			"attributes": {
				"type": record01.attributes.objectType,
				"referenceId": record01.attributes.referenceId
			},
			"LastName": record01.LastName,
			"Email": record01.Email,
			"Title": record01.Title
		})
	}
})
]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<salesforce-composite:create-sobject-tree
      doc:name="Create SObject tree"
      config-ref="Salesforce_Composite_Salesforce_Composite"
      type="Account"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Pre-Query Example

This example provides metadata for an object to query, generates two subrequests that contain this
metadata, and then executes the subrequests:

- HTTP **Listener**

  Accepts data from HTTP requests

- **Transform Message**

  Specifies a name for the object

- **Pre create**

  Provides metadata for the object to be created and generates a subrequest used in execution

- **Transform Message**

  Specifies values for fields

- **Pre query**

  Provides metadata for the object that is going to be queried and generates a subrequest used in
  execution

- **Transform Message**

  Passes output from **Pre query** operation

- **Execute composite batch**

  Sends all subrequests in one batch

- **Transform Message**

  Outputs the results in JSON format

![Studio flow with an HTTP Listener component, Transform components, and multiple Salesfore Composite operations](_images/salesforce-composite-prequery-flow.png)

### Add an HTTP Listener

The HTTP **Listener** source operation initiates the flow when it detects an event in the
`executeFlow` path. To add the listener and trigger the flow, follow these steps:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `http` and select the **Listener** operation.
3.  Drag the **Listener** operation onto the canvas.
4.  In the Listener properties window, set the **Path** field to `/executeFlow`.

### Add the First Transform Message Component

The first Transform Message component specifies a name for the created object and passes the name to
the **Pre create** operation. To add the component, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for **Transform Message**.
2.  Drag **Transform Message** onto the canvas, to the right of **Listener**.
3.  Enter the name `MyNewAccount`:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    %output application/java
    ---
    {
       Name: "MyNewAccount"
    }
    ```

### Add the Pre Create Operation

The Pre Create operation provides metadata for creating an object called `NewAccount` and generates
a subrequest for the `executeCompositeBatch` operation. To add the operation, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `salesforce` and select the Salesforce Composite **Pre
    create** operation.
2.  Drag the **Pre create** operation to the right of **Transform Message**.
3.  Click the green plus icon (**+**) to the right of the **Connector configuration** field to
    access the Salesforce Composite global element configuration fields.
4.  In the **Pre create** properties window, enter `Account` in the **Type** field.
5.  In the properties file `src/main/mule/mule-app.properties`, declare and enter values for the
    variables in the global element.

![Salesforce Composite Connector global element configuration window](_images/salesforce-composite-global-config.png)

### Add the Second Transform Message Component

The second Transform Message component specifies values for the **ID** and **Type** fields, and then
passes these values to the **Pre query** operation. To add the component, follow these steps:

1.  Drag a second **Transform Message** component to the right of **Pre create**.
2.  Enter the following name-value pairs:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    %output application/java
    ---
    {
       "Id": payload.Id,
       "Fields" : ["Id", "Name"],
       "Type": "Account"
    }
    ```

### Add the Pre Query Operation

The Pre Query operation provides metadata for an object to be queried. It generates a subrequest for
the **Execute composite batch** operation. To add the operation, follow these steps:

1.  Drag a Salesforce Composite **Pre query** operation to the right of the second **Transform
    Message**.
2.  In the **Query** field, enter the following:

    `Select Name from Account WHERE Name LIKE '%:name %'`

### Add the Third Transform Message Component

The third Transform Message operation passes the output of the **Pre query** operation to the
**Execute composite batch** operation. To add the component, follow these steps:

1.  Drag a third **Transform Message** component to the right of **Pre query**.
2.  Set the output to `payload`:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    %output application/java
    ---
    [
    payload
    ]
    ```

### Add the Execute Composite Batch Operation

The Execute Composite Batch operation executes the subrequests that the Pre Create and Pre Query
operations created.

To add the operation, drag a Salesforce Composite **Execute composite batch** operation to the right
of the third **Transform Message**.

### Add the Fourth Transform Message Component

The fourth Transform Message operation converts the output to JSON format. To add the component,
follow these steps:

1.  Drag a fourth **Transform Message** component to the right of **Execute composite batch**.
2.  Set the output to `application/json`:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    %output application/json
    ---
    payload
    ```

### XML for the Pre-Query Example

Paste this code into your XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example to your Mule app. If
needed, change the values to reflect your environment.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:salesforce-composite="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-composite" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
   xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
   xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-composite http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-composite/current/mule-salesforce-composite.xsd">
   <http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
      <http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
   </http:listener-config>
   <salesforce-composite:composite-config name="Salesforce_Composite_Config" doc:name="Salesforce Composite Config" >
      <salesforce-composite:oauth-user-pass-connection consumerKey="${consumerKey}" consumerSecret="${consumerSecret}" username="${username}" password="${password}" securityToken="${securityToken}" />
   </salesforce-composite:composite-config>
   <flow name="composite-prequery-exampleFlow" >
      <http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/executeFlow"/>
      <ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
         <ee:message >
            <ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
{
   Name: "MyNewAccount"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
         </ee:message>
      </ee:transform>
      <salesforce-composite:pre-create type="Account" doc:name="Pre create" config-ref="Salesforce_Composite_Config">
      </salesforce-composite:pre-create>
      <ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
         <ee:message >
            <ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[output application/java
---
{
   "Id": payload.Id,
   "Fields" : ["Id", "Name"],
   "Type": "Account"
}]]></ee:set-payload>
         </ee:message>
      </ee:transform>
      <salesforce-composite:pre-query doc:name="Pre query" config-ref="Salesforce_Composite_Config">
         <salesforce-composite:query >Select Name from Account WHERE Name LIKE '%:name %'
                    </salesforce-composite:query>
         <salesforce-composite:parameters ><![CDATA[#[output application/java
---
{
   "name" : "MyNewAccount"
}]]]></salesforce-composite:parameters>
      </salesforce-composite:pre-query>
      <ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
         <ee:message >
            <ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
[
   payload
]]]></ee:set-payload>
         </ee:message>
      </ee:transform>
      <salesforce-composite:execute-composite-batch doc:name="Execute composite batch" config-ref="Salesforce_Composite_Config"/>
      <ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
         <ee:message >
            <ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
         </ee:message>
      </ee:transform>
   </flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Salesforce Composite Connector Reference](salesforce-composite-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Salesforce Composite Connector 3.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

---

### Default Configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#composite-config_config-with-oauth">OAuth v2.0</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#composite-config_oauth-client-credentials">OAuth Client Credentials</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#composite-config_oauth-jwt">OAuth JWT</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#composite-config_oauth-saml">OAuth SAML</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#composite-config_oauth-user-pass">OAuth Username Password</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#composite-config_token-connection">Token Connection</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Connection Types

### OAuth v2.0

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#tls-configuration">TLS Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, then, at a minimum, the user must configure the key store in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname of the proxy. Required if a proxy is used. If this property is not set, then no proxy is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy. If the Host is set, then this property is required and the value cannot be a negative number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to authenticate against the proxy. If this property is not set, then no authentication is used against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time until a response is received. This attribute works in tandem with Response Timeout Unit. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use for the Response Timeout parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with Connection Timeout Time Unit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth consumerKey as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth consumerSecret as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorization Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The service providerâs authorization endpoint URL. The default is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Token Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The service providerâs accessToken endpoint URL. The default is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scopes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the dance. If not provided, it defaults to the scopes provided in the annotation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resource Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The resource owner ID that each component uses if it is not otherwise referenced.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Before</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>After</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a flow to execute immediately after an accessToken is received.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Listener Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A reference to a <code>&lt;http:listener-config /&gt;</code> to use to create the listener that receives the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorize Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External Callback Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If the callback endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through a non direct URL, use this parameter to tell the OAuth provider the URL to use to access the callback.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If an object store is not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default object store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth Client Credentials

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#tls-configuration">TLS Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, then, at a minimum, the user must configure the key store in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname of the proxy. Required if a proxy is used. If this property is not set, then no proxy is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy. If the Host is set, then this property is required and the value cannot be a negative number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to authenticate against the proxy. If this property is not set, then no authentication is used against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time until a response is received. This attribute works in tandem with Response Timeout Unit. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use for the Response Timeout parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with Connection Timeout Time Unit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth client secret as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The service providerâs token endpoint URL. The default is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scopes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the dance. If not provided, it defaults to the scopes provided in the annotation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If an object store is not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default object store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth JWT

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#tls-configuration">TLS Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, then, at a minimum, the user must configure the key store in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname of the proxy. Required if a proxy is used. If this property is not set, then no proxy is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy. If the Host is set, then this property is required and the value cannot be a negative number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to authenticate against the proxy. If this property is not set, then no authentication is used against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time until a response is received. This attribute works in tandem with Response Timeout Unit. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use for the Response Timeout parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with Connection Timeout Time Unit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the key store used to sign data during authentication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password of the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the certificate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username of the Salesforce user on whose behalf to take action.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL pointing to the server responsible for providing the authentication token. The default is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code>.</p></div><div><p>If implementation is for a community, <code>https://acme.force.com/customers/services/oauth2/token</code>, where <code>acme.force.com/customers</code> is your community URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Audience Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The audience identifies the authorization server as an intended audience. The authorization server must verify that it is an intended audience for the token.</p></div><div><p>Use the authorization serverâs URL for the audience value: <code>https://login.salesforce.com</code>. For testing, use: <code>https://test.salesforce.com</code>. For a community implementation, use: <code>https://community.force.com/customers</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth SAML

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#tls-configuration">TLS Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, then, at a minimum, the user must configure the key store in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname of the proxy. Required if a proxy is used. If this property is not set, then no proxy is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy. If the Host is set, then this property is required and the value cannot be a negative number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to authenticate against the proxy. If this property is not set then no authentication is used against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time until a response is received. This attribute works in tandem with Response Timeout Unit. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use for the Response Timeout parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with Connection Timeout Time Unit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the key store used to sign data during authentication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password of the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the certificate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username of the Salesforce user on whose behalf to take action.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL pointing to the server responsible for providing the authentication token. The default is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code>.</p></div><div><p>If implementation is for a community, <code>https://acme.force.com/customers/services/oauth2/token</code>, where <code>acme.force.com/customers</code> is your community URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth Username Password

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#tls-configuration">TLS Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, then, at a minimum, the user must configure the key store in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname of the proxy. Required if a proxy is used. If this property is not set, then no proxy is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy. If the Host is set, then this property is required and the value cannot be a negative number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to authenticate against the proxy. If this property is not set, then no authentication is used against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time until a response is received. This attribute works in tandem with Time Unit. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use for the Response Timeout parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with Connection Timeout Time Unit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for Salesforce-connected app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your applicationâs client secret (consumer secret in Remote Access Detail).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to initialize the session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate the user</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Userâs security token. It can be omitted if your IP has been allowlisted on Salesforce.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL pointing to the server responsible for providing the authentication token. The default is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code>.</p></div><div><p>If implementation is for a community, <code>https://acme.force.com/customers/services/oauth2/token</code>, where <code>acme.force.com/customers</code> is your community URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Token Connection

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Session ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instance Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Instance URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#tls-configuration">TLS Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a configuration for TLS, which can be used from both the client and server sides to secure communication for the Mule app. When using the HTTPS protocol, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, then, at a minimum, the user must configure the key store in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the <code>listener-config</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>HTTP</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname of the proxy. Required if a proxy is used. If this property is not set, then no proxy is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy. If <strong>Host</strong> is set, then this property is required and the value cannot be a negative number.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to authenticate against the proxy. If this property is not set, then no authentication is used against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>5</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response timeout unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Socket connection timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [Create Sobject Collections](#createSobjectCollections)
- [Create sObject Tree](#createSobjectTree)
- [Delete sObject Collections](#deleteSobjectCollections)
- [Execute Composite Batch](#executeCompositeBatch)
- [Execute Composite Graph](#executeCompositeGraph)
- [Execute Composite Request](#executeCompositeRequest)
- [Get sObject Collections](#getSobjectCollections)
- [Post Create](#postCreate)
- [Post Delete](#postDelete)
- [Post Get Limits](#postGetLimits)
- [Post Query](#postQuery)
- [Post Query All](#postQueryAll)
- [Post Retrieve](#postRetrieve)
- [Post Search](#postSearch)
- [Post Update](#postUpdate)
- [Pre Create](#preCreate)
- [Pre Delete](#preDelete)
- [Pre Get Limits](#preGetLimits)
- [Pre Query](#preQuery)
- [Pre Query All](#preQueryAll)
- [Pre Retrieve](#preRetrieve)
- [Pre Search](#preSearch)
- [Pre Update](#preUpdate)
- [Unauthorize](#unauthorize)
- [Update sObject Collections](#updateSobjectCollections)
- [Upsert sObject Collections](#upsertSobjectCollections)

### Create Sobject Collections

`<salesforce-composite:create-sobject-collections>`

Add up to 200 records and return a list of `SaveResult` objects. You can roll back the entire
request if an error occurs.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The request body.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#error-mapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type a</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#composite-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:INVALID_SESSION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSACTION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSFORMATION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:UNKNOWN
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:VALIDATION

### Create sObject Tree

`<salesforce-composite:create-sobject-tree>`

Sends a list of sObject trees to Salesforce to be created.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SObject Root Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The sObject type of the root of the trees.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SObject Tree</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of maps containing representing the list of trees that need to be created.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#error-mapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#composite-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:INVALID_SESSION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSACTION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSFORMATION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:UNKNOWN
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:VALIDATION

### Delete sObject Collections

`<salesforce-composite:delete-sobject-collections>`

Delete up to 200 records and return a list of `DeleteResult` objects. You can roll back the entire
request if an error occurs.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The IDs of the entities to be deleted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>All Or None</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to roll back the entire request when the deletion of any object fails.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#error-mapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type a</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#composite-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:INVALID_SESSION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSACTION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSFORMATION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:UNKNOWN
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:VALIDATION

### Execute Composite Batch

`<salesforce-composite:execute-composite-batch>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sub Requests</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The list of subrequests to execute.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#error-mapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#composite-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:INVALID_SESSION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSACTION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSFORMATION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:UNKNOWN
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:VALIDATION

### Execute Composite Graph

`<salesforce-composite:execute-composite-graph>`

Performs composite requests by executing a series of REST API requests in a single call.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Graphs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of graphs to execute</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#error-mapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#composite-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:VALIDATION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSACTION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSFORMATION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:UNKNOWN
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:INVALID_SESSION

### Execute Composite Request

`<salesforce-composite:execute-composite-request>`

Execute a series of REST API requests in a single call. You can use the output of one request as the
input to a subsequent request. The response bodies and HTTP statuses of the requests are returned in
a single response body. The entire request counts as a single call toward your API limits.

> [!NOTE] You can add `httpHeaders` within `compositeRequest`, for example:
> `"httpHeaders" : { "X-REQUEST-ID" : "ReferenceId" }`.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Request Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Request body</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#error-mapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#composite-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:INVALID_SESSION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSACTION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSFORMATION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:UNKNOWN
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:VALIDATION

### Get sObject Collections

`<salesforce-composite:get-sobject-collections>`

Retrieve one or more records of the same object type.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The sObject type of the root of the trees.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>List of ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of one or more IDs of the objects to return. All IDs must belong to the same object type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>List of fields</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A list of fields to include in the response.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#error-mapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type a</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type a</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#composite-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:INVALID_SESSION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSACTION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:TRANSFORMATION
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:UNKNOWN
- SALESFORCE-COMPOSITE:VALIDATION

### Post Create

`<salesforce-composite:post-create>`

Provide metadata for the result of a create. This operation takes as input one record from the
result returned by `executeCompositeBatch(String, java.util.List)`, which belongs to a create
subrequest generated by `preCreate(String, java.util.Map)`.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Create Result</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The result of the create subrequest.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output.
